race.comment: These full-size fields will make finding winners very difficult, but the flip side will be getting value. A BM85 over 1600m is a minefield, and we like NODACHI (15) has found form again since joining the Matt Dunn stable, and she will be chasing a hat-trick of wins. A wet track is no problem, and she can get the job done again. KALUAKOI (18) should secure the run of the race for the pole position, and his record here is strong. He may even try to take them all the way, especially with Zac Purton stepping into the irons. ECLAIR AWESOME (12) has been in good form, and she performs best with some cut in the ground. Ben Thompson is back in the saddle, ad despite a wide gate, she can still run another honest race. Each way. CRANKY HARRY (4) needed his fresh run, and he usually is much better second up. A good draw and having Nash Rawiller in the saddle, makes him a top 3 chance. DIAMOND EPIC (13) is also not out of this at odds.
